reasoning: The ECB deposit facility rate is currently 2.000% as of January 18, 2026. The ECB sets interest rates at scheduled monetary policy meetings. The first meeting of 2026 is scheduled for February 5, so there is essentially zero chance of a rate increase by the first resolution date of January 25, 2026. For the second resolution date (February 17, 2026), the rate will depend on the outcome of the February 5 meeting. Market consensus overwhelmingly expects the ECB to keep rates on hold at 2.0% in early 2026; a Reuters poll from mid-December showed 75% of economists expecting rates to remain flat through the entire year. While a subset of the market is pricing in a possible hike by late 2026, an abrupt increase as early as February is highly improbable without prior forward guidance, leaving only a negligible chance of an emergency or unexpected hike.
